# Memory Bank

A grep-searchable knowledge base of gotchas, decisions, issues, patterns, and data facts for ADO Companion, accumulated across sessions. Shared by all agents.

**Format:** one line per entry — `[tag] topic: description`. Use kebab-case topics. Be concise, specific, and factual. See `.github/skills/memory-bank/SKILL.md` for the full workflow.

**Tags:** `[gotcha]` (will bite you), `[decision]` (deliberate choice + rationale), `[issue]` (bug + fix), `[perf]` (perf insight), `[pattern]` (convention to follow), `[data]` (fact about deps/data).

# ── Build & Tooling ──

[decision] stack: UI is SolidJS + Fluent Web Components on WXT — azure-devops-ui is React-16-only and Fluent React v9 is heavier; Solid pairs cleanly with framework-agnostic web components.
[pattern] tsconfig-compose: extension tsconfig uses extends array ["../../tsconfig.base.json", "./.wxt/tsconfig.json"] (WXT last so its module/jsx win) + direct jsx:preserve/jsxImportSource:solid-js + include ["src",".wxt"].
[gotcha] wxt-prepare-types: tsc and type-aware oxlint need WXT's generated .wxt types — run `wxt prepare` first (postinstall and build's first step handle it).
[decision] imports-off: WXT auto-imports are disabled (imports:false) so all APIs are imported explicitly, consistent with verbatimModuleSyntax.

# ── WXT ──

[gotcha] wxt-import-paths: WXT 0.20 — defineConfig from 'wxt' (NOT 'wxt/config'); defineBackground from 'wxt/utils/define-background'; defineContentScript from 'wxt/utils/define-content-script'; createShadowRootUi from 'wxt/utils/content-script-ui/shadow-root'.
[gotcha] firefox-mv2-default: WXT builds Firefox as MV2 by default (.output/firefox-mv2); Chrome/Edge are MV3. Set manifestVersion config to target Firefox MV3.
[data] host-perms: content script + host_permissions target https://dev.azure.com/* and https://*.visualstudio.com/*.
[gotcha] content-script-single-file: WXT bundles each content script into ONE file (inlineDynamicImports) — `import()` inside the content script gets inlined, NOT code-split. So lazy-loading heavy deps (markdown-it+DOMPurify ≈ content.js ~218KB) off the content script is not possible without a disproportionate refactor (main-world injectScript / messaging). Verified: dynamic import left content.js unchanged. Also DON'T narrow `matches` to PR-only to cut load: content scripts inject on full page load, not SPA nav, so the script must already be present (broad ADO match) before a client-side navigation INTO a PR — narrowing would break SPA detection. Accept the ~218KB on every ADO page; revisit only via main-world injection if it becomes a real problem.
[pattern] heavy-dep-vendor: to load heavy libs (mermaid ~3MB, highlight.js ~160KB) on demand without bloating the single-file content script — esbuild-bundle each to ONE self-contained ESM file at BUILD time (scripts/bundle-vendor.mjs builds them → public/vendor/*.mjs, gitignored, prepended to dev/build/zip scripts, idempotent via a node_modules/.cache/vendor-versions.json stamp), mark `vendor/*` as web_accessible_resources (wxt.config), and `import(getURL('/vendor/<lib>.mjs'))` from the content script's ISOLATED world the first time it's needed. Verified live: works in MV3, no CSP issue, content.js stayed ~222KB. Note esbuild has no `inlineDynamicImports` option — `splitting:false` (default) already emits one file. Sync APIs (e.g. markdown-it's `highlight`) can't use a lazily-loaded lib, so highlight code blocks in a POST-RENDER DOM pass (like mermaid) instead.
[gotcha] wxt-geturl-type: WXT's `getURL` augmentation may not merge into the runtime type (tsc: "Property 'getURL' does not exist on WxtRuntime…") even though `.wxt/types/paths.d.ts` declares it and the public path is listed. The method exists at runtime — cast `browser.runtime as unknown as { getURL(p: string): string }`.

# ── UI (Solid + Fluent) ──

[gotcha] solid-shadow-events: use on:click (not onClick) for Fluent web components — Solid delegates onClick at the document root and misses Shadow DOM (events are retargeted).
[pattern] fluent-register: register a Fluent element via side-effect import '@fluentui/web-components/<name>/define.js', then add a Solid JSX typing for the tag in src/fluent.d.ts.
[pattern] shadow-theme: in content scripts apply the Fluent theme to the shadow container — setTheme(webLightTheme, container) — not just to document.
[data] fluent-versions: @fluentui/web-components pinned 3.0.0-rc.24 (RC), @fluentui/tokens 1.0.0-alpha.23 (alpha) — pre-GA; bump deliberately.

# ── Testing ──

[decision] coverage-scope: coverage thresholds (85/80/85/85) are enforced on src/lib; src/entrypoints and src/app are excluded as DOM/extension glue.
[pattern] test-browser: tests use WxtVitest + fakeBrowser from 'wxt/testing'; fakeBrowser.reset() runs in src/test-setup.ts; console.error/warn are suppressed there.
[pattern] solid-component-tests: .spec.tsx component tests use @solidjs/testing-library; vitest.config.ts adds vite-plugin-solid + resolve.conditions ['development','browser'] so JSX transforms (WxtVitest alone does NOT). Call cleanup() in an explicit afterEach (globals:false → no auto-cleanup). Shared fixtures in src/app/review/fixtures.ts. Examples: CommentItem/CommentCard/CommentComposer.spec.tsx.
[gotcha] test-unbound-method: oxlint flags `const {getByText}=render()` and `expect(mod.fn)` as unbound-method. Use `screen.getByText(…)` and import mocked fns by name (`import {fn}; expect(fn)`), not member access.

# ── Releasing ──

[pattern] release-stable-names: release.yml copies the versioned chrome zip to the stable name ado-companion-chrome.zip and attaches the Mozilla-signed Firefox build as ado-companion-firefox.xpi (falls back to the unsigned ado-companion-firefox.zip when AMO signing is skipped) so the docs /releases/latest/download links stay valid.
[gotcha] manifest-version-semver: manifest versions cannot carry semver pre-release tags (e.g. 1.2.0-beta.0) — use plain x.y.z.
[gotcha] firefox-signing: release/beta Firefox refuses UNSIGNED add-ons with the misleading "appears to be corrupt" error (NOT a packaging bug — the zip is valid). The GitHub-release Firefox artifact must be a Mozilla-signed .xpi: (1) set a stable `browser_specific_settings.gecko.id` (ado-companion@mjuma.github.io) emitted FIREFOX-ONLY via the wxt.config `manifest: ({ browser }) => …` fn (Chrome warns on the key); (2) `web-ext sign --channel unlisted --source-dir .output/firefox-mv2` (pnpm script sign:firefox) in release.yml, guarded by `if: env.AMO_JWT_ISSUER != ''` using repo secrets AMO_JWT_ISSUER/AMO_JWT_SECRET mapped to web-ext's env WEB_EXT_API_KEY/WEB_EXT_API_SECRET. The signed .xpi installs on normal Firefox (about:addons → Install Add-on From File, or drag onto the page). Get creds at addons.mozilla.org → Developer Hub → Manage API Keys. Unsigned local builds load via about:debugging (temporary).
[gotcha] amo-creds-whitespace: AMO returning "Upload failed: Unauthorized {detail: Error decoding signature}" from `web-ext sign` means the api key/secret is wrong/swapped OR carries a trailing newline/space (a common `gh secret set` pitfall). release.yml trims both (`tr -d '[:space:]'`) before signing and logs their lengths + an `issuer starts with user:` check (non-secret diagnostics). Fixing the whitespace made signing succeed.
[gotcha] amo-version-immutable: AMO won't re-sign an already-signed version — if the release job fails AFTER `web-ext sign` succeeds (e.g. a later step), you CANNOT re-run the same version (re-sign errors "version exists"); bump the version instead. web-ext signing took ~6 min for the 3.7MB add-on, so the sign step is the slow part of the release.
[gotcha] release-notes-quoting: release.yml passes CHANGELOG notes to `gh release create` via an ENV var (`NOTES: ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.notes }}` then `--notes "$NOTES"`), NOT inlined as `--notes "${{ steps.changelog.outputs.notes }}"`. GitHub substitutes `${{ }}` into the script TEXT, so backticks in notes (e.g. `.xpi`, `about:addons`) get command-substituted by bash → "command not found" → release fails. Bit v0.6.1 (the first release whose notes contained backticks); env-var expansion doesn't re-evaluate backticks in the value.
[pattern] firefox-autoupdate: self-distributed (unlisted) Firefox add-ons auto-update via a hosted update manifest, NOT AMO. The Firefox manifest sets `browser_specific_settings.gecko.update_url` → `…/releases/latest/download/updates.json`; release.yml generates `updates.json` (via `jq`: addon-id → updates[{version, update_link to the release .xpi, update_hash "sha256:…"}]) and uploads it as a release asset, so `latest/download` always serves the newest. Firefox polls ~daily (or about:addons → ⚙ → Check for Updates) and installs the signed `.xpi`. BOOTSTRAP: only works from the FIRST version that ships update_url (added after v0.6.2) — earlier installs need one manual update.
[gotcha] chrome-no-autoupdate: Chrome/Edge CANNOT auto-update off-store extensions for normal users — Chrome blocks installing self-hosted `.crx` (only enterprise policy can), and "Load unpacked" never auto-updates. The only path to Chrome/Edge auto-update is the Chrome Web Store / Edge Add-ons (the store handles updates). So a self-hosted updates.json is Firefox-only.
[decision] landing-site: the GitHub Pages site is a plain static landing page (`site/index.html` + `site/styles.css`, no build) deployed by `.github/workflows/deploy-site.yml` (upload-pages-artifact → deploy-pages). VitePress was REMOVED (deleted `docs/`, the `vitepress` catalog dep, and the `docs:*` root scripts). Download buttons link to the stable release assets (Firefox → the signed `.xpi`); relative asset paths so it works under the `/ado-companion/` Pages base.

# ── Review feature (PR Markdown review) ──

[decision] surface: native "Review" pivot beside Raw content/Preview on a PR .md view; in-page Shadow DOM island + Word-style comment rail; reusable surface-enhancer framework; PR-only. See @lazy-instructions/review-feature.md.
[decision] anchoring: line/block anchoring for everyone (interoperable with native ADO comments — line-based threadContext is the universal contract); phrase-highlight via thread properties + true char offsets are later additive layers.
[data] ado-pr-dom: Raw/Preview is a bolt-split-button in `.repos-compare-toolbar`; file content sibling `.repos-changes-explorer-splitter`; ADO rendered preview `.markdown-content`; PR page root `.repos-pr-details-page`. (Verified on PR 980523.)
[data] ado-view-switcher: the view switcher is a `.bolt-split-button` (main `.bolt-split-button-main` = current view; chevron `.bolt-split-button-option` opens the menu). The menu is a portal `table#__bolt-changeViewerMode` (role=menu) of `tr.bolt-menuitem-row` items (ids `__bolt-diffOrientationSideBySide/Inline`, etc.); each row has an icon cell `.bolt-menuitem-cell-icon .fabric-icon.ms-Icon--*` and a text cell `.bolt-menuitem-cell-text`. Inject a cloned `<tr>` "Review" here. (Verified live on PR 1002836.)
[data] ado-splitter-panes: `.repos-changes-explorer-splitter` has 3 children — `.vss-Splitter--pane-fixed` (the file tree, ~295px, KEEP), `.vss-Splitter--divider`, and `.vss-Splitter--pane-flexible.relative` (the file content, full width). Mount the Review island as an absolute overlay inside the flexible pane (it's position:relative) so the tree stays and the review fills the content width. (Verified live on PR 1002836.)
[gotcha] hide-native-via-visibility: while the Review island overlays the flex pane, hide ADO's native content with `visibility:hidden` applied via a CLASS on the pane (light-DOM `<style>`, selector `.host > :not([data-ado-companion=review-island])`), toggled on activate/deactivate. NOT display:none (breaks ADO's layout/measure → blank pane on exit) and NOT a bare opaque overlay (ADO's inline comment threads have higher stacking and bleed through). visibility keeps layout intact, and a class toggle restores everything with no per-element tracking.
[data] ado-discussionId: ADO focuses a specific PR comment via the `discussionId` URL query param (also used for comment permalinks / clicking a comment in the tree). ReviewView reads it and scrolls to/highlights that thread on load; CommentItem's copy-link writes `?discussionId=<threadId>`.
[pattern] feature-progress: Review MVP complete + heavily UX-refined. Native dropdown entry; content-pane overlay (tree preserved); select-text-to-comment with floating button + popover; Word-style card alignment; ADO-styled controls + icon edit/delete/copy-link; relative timestamps; confirmed deletes; sticky across file navigation. Comment pane: resizable divider, sticky non-wrapping toolbar with status + by-person filters, likes/collapse, and prev/next comment navigation (wraps, respects filters). Doc rendering: relative/LFS images, commented-text-span highlight, mermaid diagrams, and code syntax highlighting (lazy-loaded vendored bundle, themed light/dark, sanitized SVG; src/app/review/mermaid.ts). Settings/allowlist gate the host.
[pattern] sticky-review: the reconcile key includes filePath, so ADO file navigation remounts the enhancer per file. Keep "Review mode" on by persisting intent in a module-level `stickyPrId` (set on activate, cleared only when the user picks another native view), and auto-reactivating on mount once `.vss-Splitter--pane-flexible` exists. deactivate(clearIntent) distinguishes user-exit (clear) from remount cleanup (preserve). The island tags itself `data-file` for debugging/verification.
[gotcha] composer-key-hijack: ADO's document-level key handlers steal typing to the global search when no input it recognizes is focused (our textarea is in a shadow DOM, so document.activeElement is the host div). Fix = autofocus the composer textarea on open (onMount + rAF/timeout retries) AND stop key events propagating out of the island host; verified typing (incl. shortcut keys) stays in the box.
[gotcha] ado-menu-synthetic-click: opening ADO's bolt split-button menu via element.click() is flaky in automation — use a real pointer click (page.mouse.click at the element's center) to reliably open it and to click the injected Review row.
[gotcha] shadow-dom-icons: ADO's fabric icon font (ms-Icon--*) does NOT render inside our Shadow DOM island — the @font-face is document-scoped. Use inline SVG (src/app/review/Icons.tsx, currentColor) for island icons; fabric-icon classes only work in light-DOM injections (e.g. the cloned view-menu row + the split-button main label).
[gotcha] duplicate-split-button: a draft PR page has multiple `.bolt-split-button-main` (the "Publish" button + the view switcher) — always scope view-switcher queries to `.repos-compare-toolbar`.
[pattern] rail-card-alignment: align comment cards to their anchored text (Word-style) by setting each card's absolute `top` to `block.getBoundingClientRect().top - rail.getBoundingClientRect().top` (scroll-independent: both shift equally), sorted with downward collision stacking; recompute via a ResizeObserver on the doc + each card slot and on window resize. The doc + rail share one scroll container so positions stay aligned while scrolling.
[data] settings: ReviewSettings { enabled, allowlist } in src/lib/settings; isUrlAllowed gates the content host (empty allowlist = run on all matched ADO hosts; else URL host+path must contain an entry). Persisted via WXT storage.defineItem('local:reviewSettings'). The `options` entrypoint registers options_ui; the popup calls browser.runtime.openOptionsPage().
[gotcha] playwright-extension-pages: playwright-cli only navigates http/https/about/data — it CANNOT open chrome-extension:// or edge:// pages and can't click the toolbar action, so popup/options pages can't be auto-driven (verify via build + manual, or pnpm dev:extension). Edge's default profile also allows only one instance — the user's own Edge holding it blocks a persistent-profile launch ("Opening in existing browser session").
[data] native-threads: threads created via createThread with a line-based threadContext are real native ADO PR comments — verified live (resolving one through our UI flipped ADO's own "All comments resolved"). Our write path is interoperable, not a parallel store.
[gotcha] connectiondata-preview: the org `_apis/connectionData` endpoint rejects `api-version=7.1` with HTTP 400 ("must supply -preview flag") — use `7.1-preview`. fetchCurrentUser uses it; guard the currentUser resource with .catch so a failure can't blank the Review render.
[gotcha] mentions-encoding: ADO comment mentions are stored in content as `@<GUID>` (uppercase identity GUID = author.id format). markdown-it/DOMPurify will mangle the `<…>`, so pre-process `@<GUID>` before renderMarkdown — `renderMentions` (src/lib/review/mentions.ts) replaces each token with a highlighted `<span class="acr-mention">@Name</span>` (name HTML-escaped; the span+class survive markdown-it html:true + DOMPurify). Styled like ADO (`.acr-mention`: communication-foreground text + subtle blue pill). The IdentityPicker search (`{org}/_apis/IdentityPicker/Identities`, POST) is same-origin and works; resolving a GUID→name via vssps `_apis/identities` is CORS-blocked from the content script. Names only resolve for cached identities (mention picks + comment authors), else falls back to `@mention`.
[pattern] likes-tooltip: a comment's like button (`.acr-like`) tooltip lists every liker's displayName one-per-line (`usersLiked[].displayName` joined by `\n` — native title tooltips honor `\n`), falling back to "Like" when none. usersLiked is IdentityRef[] (has displayName) from listThreads.
[gotcha] extension-reload: a page reload does NOT pick up a rebuilt extension — the browser keeps the version loaded at launch. Relaunch the browser (or reload the extension) after `wxt build` to test changes via playwright-cli.
[gotcha] resource-refetch-teardown: createResource `refetch()` flips `resource.loading` true, so a `<Show when={!resource.loading}>` gating the list TEARS DOWN + remounts the whole subtree on every refetch (visible flash; resets child state like collapse). Fix: mirror the resource into a `createStore` + `reconcile(list,{key:'id'})` (preserves node identity → in-place patch), and gate the loading fallback on `loading && store.length===0` (initial only) so refetches keep the list mounted. This is what makes likes/status changes flash-free and keeps resolved threads from re-collapsing.
[data] mention-avatars: ADO's IdentityPicker no longer returns an `image` (or populated `subjectDescriptor`) for candidates — build the avatar from the GUID: `${organizationUrl}/_api/_common/identityImage?id=${localId}` (200 image/png, cookie-auth, same-origin; verified live). GraphProfile/MemberAvatars needs a descriptor and 404s on null.
[gotcha] sticky-in-padded-scroll: a `position:sticky; top:0` header inside a scroll container with `padding-top` pins at the container's CONTENT top (below the padding), so absolutely-positioned siblings can scroll into the top-padding band and appear ABOVE the header. Fix: remove the scroll container's `padding-top` (move it onto the doc/content) so the header pins flush at the very top. (Review toolbar lives inside `.acr-rail` as a sticky first child above the absolute `.acr-rail__slot` cards.)
[pattern] rail-resize: the comment pane is resizable via an `.acr-divider` (col-resize) between `.acr-doc` and `.acr-rail`; drag updates a `railWidth` signal applied as inline `flex:0 0 {n}px`. Ephemeral by design (signal, not persisted → resets to 340 on reload). Dragging is clamped to keep a min doc width; widening the rail reflows the doc so the ResizeObserver re-aligns cards.
[data] ado-thread-properties: thread `properties` use the VSS PropertiesCollection format `{ "<key>": { "$type": "System.String", "$value": "..." } }` (native keys: Microsoft.TeamFoundation.Discussion.SupportsMarkdown=Int32 1, …UniqueID=String). Sending custom props on createThread works (verified live on a real PR). Native md comments also store source column offsets in threadContext.rightFileStart/End.offset (1-based columns in the RAW line).
[pattern] comment-text-highlight: the exact commented text span is highlighted with `<mark class="acr-hl" data-thread-id>` in addition to the block left-border. On create, store the selected rendered text as a thread property `ADOCompanion.Highlight` = JSON {q:quote,o:offset}; on render, find the quote in the anchored block and wrap it. Native/pre-existing comments: derive a single-line quote from threadContext source offsets (rawLineQuote) as best-effort (skips multi-line/whole-line). Pure helpers + tests in src/lib/markdown/highlight.ts; active state synced via .acr-hl--active.
[gotcha] native-highlight-precise-range: ADO's native file views highlight a comment's `threadContext` rightFileStart→rightFileEnd character range. Anchoring a single-line selection with offset 1→(lineLen+1) makes ADO highlight the WHOLE ROW (not the phrase). Fix: compute the precise source columns of the selected phrase — `phraseSourceRange` (src/lib/markdown/highlight.ts) locates the rendered quote text in the raw source line via findQuoteOffset and returns {startOffset,endOffset}; buildNewThreadInput uses it for single-line selections (`buildLineThreadContext` now takes startOffset+endOffset). Falls back to a degenerate line anchor (offset 1→1, no native highlight) for multi-line selections OR when the rendered quote isn't in the source line verbatim (inline code/table/formatting) — NEVER the whole-line span. The Review island's own highlight is property-driven (ADOCompanion.Highlight) so it stays precise regardless of the native range. Verified live: phrase "Split the existing monolithic" → range 1→30 (exact), not whole row.
[gotcha] shadow-scroll-composed: scroll events are composed:false and do NOT cross the Shadow DOM boundary — a document-level `addEventListener('scroll',…,true)` never fires for our shadow review pane. Bind scroll listeners to the shadow scroll container element directly (in its ref). A non-capturing listener on that element also naturally ignores nested scrollers (code blocks).
[pattern] block-thread-anchors: a rendered block carries only the FIRST thread's `data-thread-id`, so keep a `Map<threadId, blockEl>` (threadAnchors, rebuilt each anchoring pass) and use it for reposition/focus/active — otherwise later threads sharing a block lose their document anchor (querySelector by id finds only the first).
[pattern] solid-textarea-write: to set a Solid-controlled textarea/select value from outside (tests/automation), assign `.value` then dispatch a bubbling `input`/`change` event so the `on:input`/`on:change` signal updates.
[gotcha] items-raw-text: the ADO items API (file content, `includeContent=true`) returns RAW TEXT, not JSON — read it with `adoGetText`, not `adoGetJson` (items.ts).
[gotcha] theme-vars-pierce-shadow: ADO theme CSS custom properties pierce the Shadow DOM boundary — style the island with `--text-primary-color`, `--background-color`, `--palette-neutral-N` (as "R, G, B" triplets, e.g. `rgba(var(--palette-neutral-8), .5)`), `--communication-foreground`, with literal fallbacks, for native light/dark theming.
[pattern] anchor-line-to-block: markdown-it block tokens carry `.map` → emitted as `data-source-line`/`data-source-end-line` (1-based); `src/lib/markdown/anchor.ts` maps a thread's `threadContext.rightFileStart.line` to the exact or closest-preceding rendered block.
[pattern] pr-timeline-filter: the PR Overview activity timeline lives in `.activity-feed-list` (the sibling directly below `.repos-activity-filter-dropdown`, ADO's "Show everything (N)" filter). Each event is a `.bolt-timeline-row`; categorize by its MARKER (robust across PR states — text/`.repos-comment-header-persona` vary when collapsed/abandoned): `.activity-feed-timeline-icon.ms-Icon--Chat` = comment thread, `.activity-feed-timeline-icon.ms-Icon--CommentAdd` = the add-comment composer (skip), `.activity-feed-timeline-iteration` = pushed commits, dot/anything else = action. [gotcha] DON'T read the comment author from the DOM — the feed is VIRTUALIZED, so a collapsed/off-screen comment row renders only the "Write a reply" box (the CURRENT USER's avatar), not the author's; `img[src*=MemberAvatars]` then grabs the wrong identity, mis-splitting bot vs human AND flashing/looping as ADO re-renders on filter. Instead resolve authorship from the API: `listThreads` → each thread's first comment author `descriptor` (`aad.`/`msa.`=human→Comments, `svc.`/`s2s.`=service/bot→System Messages; note bot comments may be `commentType:'system'`, so DON'T filter by commentType). Each `.bolt-timeline-row` carries the event's epoch-SECONDS timestamp as a numeric child element `id`; build a `Map<epochSeconds, isBot>` over every comment's publishedDate+lastUpdatedDate and look up the row's id (±2s). 100% match on test PRs, deterministic (no flash). The `timeline-filter` enhancer (matches any PR URL via `parsePrRef`, anchor `.repos-activity-filter-dropdown`) injects a shadow-island tab strip as the dropdown's first child with `margin-right:auto` (dropdown is `display:flex; justify-end` → tabs land left, "Show everything" stays right) and hides non-matching rows via `style.display`, re-applied through a MutationObserver on the feed's parent.
[gotcha] native-view-collapsed-threads: threads our extension creates via REST render COLLAPSED (`.repos-editor-discussion-expand`) in ADO's native file views (Inline/Side-by-side/Raw) until expanded/re-fetched — NOT a payload problem (proven: a native degenerate, no-SupportsMarkdown thread renders expanded; ours rendered collapsed — the only difference is ADO learning of it via background push vs a fresh fetch). Two fixes tried: (1) re-select the open file (`.bolt-tree-row.selected`.click()) to force a re-fetch — works but re-renders the file and its settle-poll is slow on comment-heavy/big PRs (long collapsed window). (2) CURRENT: expand the thread IN PLACE — click its own `.repos-editor-discussion-expand` toggle, matched to our comment via the snippet ADO puts in the toggle's `aria-label` (`<author>: "<text>…"`). review-enhancer.tsx: `ReviewView.onThreadCreated(content)` pushes content to `pendingComments`; on leaving Review for a native view, `expandCreatedComments()` polls `.repos-editor-discussion-expand` and clicks the one whose aria-label includes the comment snippet. Verified live (manual + user): clicking the toggle expands instantly in place, no file reload, and the comment still appears in the file-tree comment list (expanding the thread makes ADO load it fully). Inherent ~300-600ms collapsed window first (ADO renders collapsed before we can click), but no slow settle-poll/re-fetch — much faster than re-select on big PRs.

# ── Pipelines tab & PR tabs ──

[data] pr-tabbar: the PR page tab strip is `div.repos-pr-details-page-tabbar` wrapping the tablist `div.bolt-tabbar-tabs` (role=tablist); each tab is `a.bolt-tab.focus-treatment.flex-noshrink` (textContent = label, `selected` class = active). The tabbar's parent `.repos-pr-details-page` (a flex-column) holds three children: `.repos-pr-header`, the tabbar, and `.page-content` (the per-tab content area). (Verified live on PR 969472.)
[data] pr-pipeline-status: to show a PR's pipeline stages/jobs in-page: GET `{orgUrl}/{project}/_apis/build/builds?branchName=refs/pull/{prId}/merge&queryOrder=queueTimeDescending&$top=100&api-version=7.1` → all builds on the PR merge ref; dedupe by `definition.id` (newest first) = every pipeline associated with the PR, with `definition.name`. Then per build GET `{orgUrl}/{project}/_apis/build/builds/{buildId}/timeline?api-version=7.1` → records typed Stage/Phase/Job/Task with state (pending|inProgress|completed) + result (succeeded|failed|skipped|canceled|abandoned|succeededWithIssues). Hierarchy Stage→Phase→Job→Task; group jobs under a stage by climbing parentId. Classic stage-less builds have only Phase/Job (collect under a synthetic "Build" stage). (The PR "Build" branch-policy evaluation `{orgUrl}/{projGuid}/_apis/policy/evaluations?artifactId=vstfs:///CodeReview/CodeReviewId/{projGuid}/{prId}` also carries `context.buildId`, but builds-by-merge-ref is simpler, named, and catches all pipelines — not just required Build policies.) Logic: src/lib/pipelines/timeline.ts (parser) + src/lib/ado/pipelines.ts (loadPrPipelines).
[pattern] pr-tab-content-swap: the Pipelines tab enhancer (src/app/pipelines/pipelines-enhancer.tsx) injects an `a.bolt-tab` "Pipelines" into `.bolt-tabbar-tabs` (the tab IS the mount marker → host re-mounts on SPA wipe) + a shadow island appended to `.repos-pr-details-page`. Match ADO's tab DOM for vertical alignment: `<a class=bolt-tab…><span class=bolt-tab-inner-container><span class=bolt-tab-text>Label</span></span></a>` (a bare text node sits at the top, misaligned). The tab starts GREYED/disabled and ENABLES once the first load confirms pipelines exist (no active→grey flash); a small overall-status badge dot (worst-wins via pipelinesOverallStatus, colored by status) lives in the tab and updates each load. Fetch on mount + auto-refresh every 60s (skipped when `document.hidden`) + a manual Refresh button in the view; a transient refresh failure keeps the last good data (only errors when there's nothing yet). Each stage/job status circle is a deep link into the build results (job → `…/_build/results?buildId={id}&view=logs&j={recordId}`, stage → `&view=results`); job/stage names are `title` tooltips. Activate = hide `.page-content` (display:none) + show island + move the `selected` class from the prev native tab to ours; a CAPTURE click listener on the tablist deactivates on any native-tab click (restores page-content + prev selection — ADO then navigates); a MutationObserver keeps page-content hidden + the island present while active. Not sticky across navigation (re-mount resets to the native view).
[pattern] per-feature-settings: settings is `CompanionSettings { enabled (master), allowlist, features:{review,timeline,pipelines}, hiddenPrTabs:[] }` (src/lib/settings/model.ts, FEATURES drives the options UI). Storage `local:reviewSettings` v1→v2 migration (migrateToV2) preserves enabled+allowlist and defaults all features on. Each SurfaceEnhancer carries `feature:FeatureId`; the host gates mounting via `featureEnabled(settings, enhancer.feature)` passed as planReconcile's `isEnabled` predicate (a disabled feature unmounts). The Pipelines feature owns `hiddenPrTabs`, enforced by the pr-tab-filter enhancer (shouldHideTab — case-insensitive, never hides "Overview").

# ── Dev loop / preview ──

[pattern] preview-auth: drive the user's authed Edge via `playwright-cli open --browser msedge --persistent --profile "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Microsoft Edge" <url>`. See the preview-in-devops skill.
[gotcha] edge-cdp-port: --remote-debugging-port is blocked on Edge's default profile; use Playwright's persistent-profile launch (pipe CDP) instead.
[pattern] hmr-loop: `pnpm dev:extension` (WXT) gives hot-reload in a separate `.dev-profile` (sign into ADO once there). Auth there is separate from the user's main Edge profile.
[gotcha] dev-runner-edge: WXT's dev runner uses chrome-launcher, which (1) only finds Google Chrome → "No Chrome installations found" on Edge-only machines, and (2) does NOT create an explicitly-passed --user-data-dir → ENOENT on `.dev-profile/chrome-out.log`. Both fixed in wxt.config.ts: `webExt.binaries` points at an Edge binary (env `WXT_BROWSER_BINARY` overrides; per-platform Edge fallback; undefined ⇒ default Chrome lookup), and the `.dev-profile` dir is mkdir'd at config load. Edge runs the chrome-mv3 build.
[gotcha] playwright-load-extension: to load the built extension into the user's authed Edge, use a gitignored `playwright-cli.local.json` (browser.launchOptions.channel=msedge + args `--load-extension`/`--disable-extensions-except` with ABSOLUTE paths to `.output/chrome-mv3`) AND pass `--persistent --profile` flags — config `userDataDir` alone yields `<in-memory>`/no auth.
[gotcha] playwright-main-world: playwright-cli `eval`/console only sees the page's main world, not the content-script isolated world — to debug the content script, surface state/errors into the DOM (e.g. read the island's `shadowRoot`).
